mainEntity.name: What income tax services does Virtual Auditor provide in Anna Salai (Mount Road), Chennai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Complete income tax lifecycle: ITR filing (ITR-1 through ITR-7), tax audit (Section 44AB), advance tax computation, TDS compliance (monthly deposit + quarterly returns), assessment response, CIT(A) appeal, and ITAT representation. FCA-led practice serving businesses and individuals across Chennai.

mainEntity.name: How much does income tax filing cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Individual ITR (salary/capital gains): ₹5,000-₹15,000. Business ITR: ₹10,000-₹50,000. Tax audit: ₹25,000-₹1,00,000. CIT(A) appeal: ₹50,000-₹2,00,000. ITAT representation: ₹1,00,000-₹5,00,000. Virtual Auditor provides bundled annual packages.

Business turnover >₹1 crore (₹10 crore if 95%+ digital transactions). Professional gross receipts >₹50 lakh. Tax audit report (3CA/3CB + 3CD) due with ITR by October 31. Non-filing: penalty of 0.5% of turnover (max ₹1.5 lakh).

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late TDS deposit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Interest: 1.5% per month from deduction date to deposit date. Penalty under Section 271C: equal to the TDS amount. Prosecution under Section 276B: imprisonment 3 months to 7 years. TDS compliance is among the most severe penalty provisions in the IT Act.

mainEntity.name: Can you handle income tax appeals for businesses in Anna Salai (Mount Road)?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Virtual Auditor provides CIT(A) appeal filing (Form 35, within 30 days of demand), ITAT appeal filing and representation, stay application for disputed demands, and penalty appeal under Section 270A/271. We represent across all income tax appellate forums.

mainEntity.name: What advance tax installments are required?

acceptedAnswer.text: Quarterly: 15% by June 15, 45% by September 15, 75% by December 15, 100% by March 15. Interest under Section 234B (non-payment) and 234C (deferment) applies on shortfalls. Virtual Auditor computes advance tax and manages quarterly payments.

mainEntity.name: Do you provide tax planning for businesses in Anna Salai (Mount Road), Chennai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — we advise on: Section 115BAB (15% rate for new manufacturing), startup deductions under Section 80-IAC, R&D deductions under Section 35, and optimal salary structuring for tax efficiency. Tax planning is most effective when done at the start of the financial year.

@graph.name: How to Get Income Tax Filing & Appeal Services in Anna Salai (Mount Road), Chennai

step.name: Tax Planning and Entity Assessment
step.text: Evaluate the applicable tax regime: regular rates (25% for companies), Section 115BAB (15% for new manufacturing), presumptive taxation (Section 44AD/44ADA for eligible businesses/professionals). Determine advance tax liability and quarterly payment schedule.

step.name: TDS Compliance Setup
step.text: Identify all TDS obligations: salary (Section 192), contractor payments (194C), professional fees (194J), rent (194I), interest (194A). Configure TDS deduction, monthly deposit by 7th of next month, and quarterly return filing (24Q for salary, 26Q for non-salary).

step.name: Advance Tax Computation and Payment
step.text: Calculate advance tax liability and pay in quarterly installments: 15% by June 15, 45% by September 15, 75% by December 15, 100% by March 15. Shortfall attracts interest under Section 234B (for failure to pay) and 234C (for deferment).

step.name: Tax Audit (Section 44AB)
step.text: Mandatory if business turnover exceeds ₹1 crore (₹10 crore if 95%+ transactions are digital) or professional gross receipts exceed ₹50 lakh. Tax audit report (Form 3CA/3CB + 3CD) filed by the ITR due date. Covers all tax-relevant disclosures.

step.name: Income Tax Return Filing
step.text: ITR-6 for companies, ITR-5 for LLPs, ITR-3 for business/professional individuals, ITR-1/2 for salaried/other individuals. Due date: October 31 (if audit applicable), July 31 (non-audit). Include all schedules, disclosures, and verification.

step.name: Assessment Response and Appeal
step.text: If the Assessing Officer issues notice/demand: respond within prescribed time. If demand confirmed: appeal before CIT(A) within 30 days. If CIT(A) adverse: appeal before ITAT within 2 months from end of month of CIT(A) order.
